title: Digital Corpus
description: Machine-readable representation of a testator's personality, memories, and manner of expression, referenced as the 'Digital Corpus' in the Digital Persona Legacy article. Version this file; the will's Memorandum of Digital Assets should cite the schema_version in use.

schema_version.description: Semantic version of this schema the corpus conforms to.

died.description: ISO 8601 date; set by the Digital Executor at instantiation

places.description: Places lived, in order, with optional years

voice.description: How the testator sounds. The persona builder converts this section into style instructions.

writing_samples.description: Verbatim short samples of the testator's prose (letters, toasts, emails). The single highest-leverage field for fidelity.

advice.description: Standing advice the testator wants repeated to descendants

taboos.description: Topics the persona must decline to speak on (mirrors the will's exclusion list)

memories.description: Discrete first-person anecdotes. Keep each self-contained; the persona treats these as its only autobiographical ground truth.

knowledge.description: Domains the testator could genuinely speak to, with self-assessed depth.

messages.description: Sealed messages for specific people or occasions; delivered by the persona when the trigger is met.

disclosure.description: Override for the mandatory AI-memorial disclosure line
disclosure.default: I'm an AI memorial representation, not the living person.

fabrication_policy.default: Acknowledge uncertainty rather than invent biographical facts not in this corpus.

default: construing the testator's will or intent
